title: Kim Manager Korean drama seed
notes: Founder QA core case. Korean drama should remain dominant even when a seed title is fuzzy.

title: Korean action option recommendation
notes: Regression case for country option behaving as weak score boost instead of real condition.

title: Japan SF fantasy recommendation
notes: Covers Japan + SF/Fantasy and content diversity checks from Sprint 8.

title: US horror movie recommendation
notes: Validates country + genre hard filtering for a globally common genre.

title: UK thriller drama recommendation
notes: Regression case for TV + UK + Thriller returning animation or fallback noise.

title: Drama Japan SF fantasy narrow filter
notes: Narrow-filter fallback case. Content type must remain hard even when genre/country is relaxed.

title: Animation Japan SF fantasy recommendation
notes: Animation-only is allowed to prioritize animation heavily.

title: Short runtime recommendation
notes: Runtime option must visibly change results.

title: Long runtime recommendation
notes: Long runtime starts at 120 minutes; 120 intentionally overlaps the medium option.

title: Data source integrity
notes: UI Data Source, API dataSource, fallbackUsed, and actual result providerId must match.

title: Multi-seed mixed taste balance
notes: Protects multi-seed balancing and seed exclusion behavior.

title: Provider fallback transparency
notes: Fallback results are allowed only when the API explicitly declares fallbackUsed.

title: Japan SF movie primary integrity
notes: All primary results must remain movies; fewer accurate same-country results are allowed.

title: Country primary result integrity
notes: Country-relaxed candidates may exist only outside the primary results array.

title: Primary franchise diversity
notes: The first 12 primary results must contain no duplicate TMDB id and no franchise more than once.

title: Japan SF drama exact genre integrity
notes: TV genre 10765 is provider-combined Sci-Fi & Fantasy metadata; diagnostics must retain that limitation.

title: Japan SF animation exact genre integrity
notes: Animation genre 16 alone must not qualify as an exact SF match.

title: US horror exact genre integrity
notes: Regression guard for a historically strong country and genre combination.

title: Primary exact ratio integrity
notes: Primary assembly may return fewer accurate results but may not exceed the relaxation ratio.

title: Seed title scoring integrity
notes: Seed-related candidates must receive titleMatch signal while the input seed itself remains excluded.

title: Multi-seed shared request budget
notes: The real product multi-seed path must share one request context and preserve contribution from at least two seeds.

title: Large multi-seed budget safety
notes: Ten unique titles may be partially deferred, but the recommendation action must remain within one 24-call budget.

title: Partial seed failure integrity
notes: Successful TMDB seed candidates must survive when another title cannot be resolved, without Mock mixing.

title: Recommendation deadline safety
notes: A fixed stalled-response fixture must stop at the shared deadline and return already collected TMDB candidates.

title: Japan SF drama live breadth
notes: TV SF uses provider-combined genre 10765 while retaining the selected genre-sf contract.

title: UK thriller drama semantic integrity
notes: TV thriller accepts Crime or Mystery provider evidence and limited semantic evidence, never plain Drama alone.

title: Adaptive search budget recycling
notes: Unresolved early titles release their unused recommendation reservations so later valid titles can be processed.

title: Input language preservation
notes: Suggestion selection preserves the typed string while sending confirmed TMDB metadata.

title: Translated duplicate seed deduplication
notes: Confirmed aliases that resolve to one media type and TMDB ID produce one candidate request group.

title: Genre top eight order
notes: The collapsed group remains stable even when provider metadata is incomplete.

title: Empty state copy integrity
notes: A selected content type with zero results must not be described as an unselected content type.

title: TV action semantic specialization
notes: TV 10759 requires action evidence before genre-action specialization.

title: TV adventure semantic specialization
notes: TV 10759 requires journey or exploration evidence before genre-adventure specialization.

title: TV action-adventure provider combined
notes: TV 10759 remains one provider-combined value when no specialization evidence exists.

title: TV SF semantic specialization
notes: TV 10765 is specialized to SF only when science-fiction evidence exists.

title: TV fantasy semantic specialization
notes: TV 10765 is specialized to Fantasy only when magic or myth evidence exists.

title: TV SF-fantasy provider combined
notes: TV 10765 remains provider-combined when no reliable specialization evidence exists.

title: TV war semantic specialization
notes: TV 10768 requires military or combat evidence before genre-war specialization.

title: TV politics semantic specialization
notes: TV 10768 requires government or political-power evidence before genre-politics specialization.

title: TV war-politics provider combined
notes: TV 10768 remains a combined provider value without reliable war or politics specialization.

title: TV romance false-positive protection
notes: Drama 18 alone never creates a Romance canonical or semantic value.

title: TV horror false-positive protection
notes: Mystery 9648 alone never creates a Horror canonical or semantic value.

title: Format taxonomy separation
notes: TV Movie, News, Reality, Talk, and Soap remain format values and never narrative genres.

title: Family and Kids audience separation
notes: Family and Kids remain separate audience preferences without additive double scoring.

title: Animation type and style integrity
notes: Animation type is a hard constraint while Animation style is one non-duplicated taxonomy signal.

title: Canonical genre single-specialization integrity
notes: One shared Provider ID does not grant every specialized canonical value in its semantic pair.

title: Actual eligible later deferred metric
notes: The metric is calculated from actual scheduler records and remaining budget rather than a constant.

title: Raw input count and resolved work count integrity
notes: Three input slots may resolve to one work while preserving raw and alias counts separately.

title: TV action semantic recall
notes: TV 10759 action specialization or controlled provider-combined evidence supplies at least eight accurate drama results.

title: TV adventure semantic recall
notes: TV 10759 adventure evidence preserves breadth without filling Primary with unrelated Drama.

title: Action-adventure semantic tie preserves both filter matches
notes: Equal action and adventure evidence may pass both filters instead of being dropped.

title: Action-adventure scoring avoids double count
notes: Multiple filter matches in one semantic family contribute only the strongest family score.

title: Drama content type uses TV provider path
notes: Drama recommendation uses TV Discover and excludes Movie candidates.

title: Movie content type excludes TV results
notes: Movie recommendation uses Movie Discover and excludes TV candidates.

title: Animation content type and style remains correct
notes: Animation remains a hard content type without duplicating the style signal.

title: Korean provider genre label presentation
notes: Known TMDB Provider genre names are presented with Korean canonical labels.

title: Selected option recommendation reason priority
notes: The card reason prioritizes the selected and actually matched option over unrelated tags.

title: Duplicate localized title primary protection
notes: Primary keeps one result for the same localized title and removes exact TMDB duplicates.

title: Action and SF OR matching metadata integrity
notes: OR matching remains active and records which selected genres actually matched.

title: Controlled combined fallback integrity
notes: Controlled 10759 fallback requires adjacent action evidence and never admits plain Drama.

title: Submitted preference snapshot remains stable after draft genre change
notes: Draft genre edits never mutate the submitted session used by existing cards.

title: Draft content type change does not mutate existing card reason
notes: Card and detail reasons remain bound to submitted content types and filters.

title: Movie and animation style excludes TV animation
notes: Animation display type preserves movie provider identity.

title: Drama and animation style excludes movie animation
notes: Animation display type preserves TV provider identity.

title: Animation content type allows movie and TV animation
notes: Animation hard type allows both provider media paths and rejects live action.

title: Disney Plus hard filter requires matching KR streaming provider
notes: KR Disney Plus flatrate/free/ads evidence is mandatory.

title: Amazon Prime Video hard filter requires matching KR streaming provider
notes: Prime Video provider 119 is verified through KR streaming availability.

title: Apple TV Plus hard filter requires matching KR streaming provider
notes: Apple TV subscription provider 350 is distinct from Apple TV Store provider 2.

title: Unknown OTT metadata excluded when OTT selected
notes: Unknown watch metadata cannot pass a selected OTT hard constraint.

title: Rent or buy only availability does not pass subscription OTT filter
notes: Apple TV Store rent-only availability is not Apple TV Plus subscription availability.

title: Unknown runtime excluded when runtime selected
notes: Runtime zero or missing cannot pass a selected runtime hard constraint.

title: Runtime mismatch excluded from Primary
notes: Post-detail runtime validation excludes mismatches from Primary.

title: Related current TMDB item excluded
notes: The current provider content identity never reappears in Related.

title: Related current normalized title excluded
notes: Localized and original title aliases of the current item are excluded.

title: Related sequel remains allowed
notes: A distinct sequel such as Moana 2 remains eligible.

title: Latest recommendation request wins
notes: A delayed earlier response cannot overwrite the latest recommendation session.

title: Latest related request wins
notes: A delayed Related response cannot attach to a newer detail item.

title: Founder QA diagnostics contain no secret value
notes: Credentials are redacted while configured state remains visible.

title: Fallback and Mock status visible in QA diagnostics
notes: The last recommendation source is distinct from the environment health check.

title: Provider Genre ID and Semantic Match visible in QA diagnostics
notes: Card diagnostics expose provider genre 10759 and its semantic match mode without secrets.

title: Runtime medium live hard filter integrity
notes: Live and deterministic paths require known movie runtime at or below 120 minutes.

title: Drama Adventure exact recall reaches available threshold
notes: Available TV adventure evidence is retrieved before any genre relaxation.

title: Drama Adventure never uses same-country genre relaxation
notes: Adventure breadth never comes from unrelated same-country drama.

title: Action candidate is not automatically Adventure
notes: TV 10759 action-only evidence cannot satisfy the adventure specialization.

title: Adventure semantic candidate receives detail allocation
notes: Provider-combined candidates receive fair detail opportunities before semantic filtering.

title: Japan SF retains SF-specialized evidence
notes: TV 10765 requires SF evidence for the specialized SF filter.

title: Japan Fantasy retains Fantasy-specialized evidence
notes: TV 10765 requires fantasy evidence for the specialized fantasy filter.

title: SF and Fantasy overlap remains within contract
notes: Specialized evidence creates distinct SF and fantasy sets without random exclusion.

title: UK War country hard constraint
notes: Additional breadth cannot relax the UK hard constraint.

title: UK Politics country hard constraint
notes: Additional breadth cannot relax the UK hard constraint.

title: US Horror semantic precision
notes: Mystery 9648 alone remains insufficient for TV horror.

title: Movie Seed plus Movie and Drama collects both media types
notes: A movie seed reserves a related TV collection path when drama is selected.

title: Two-type exact pool reserves at least three per available type
notes: Final assembly reserves coverage before filling remaining score-ranked slots.

title: Three-type exact pool reserves at least two per available type
notes: Animation counts as one display type while movie and TV provider paths remain distinct.

title: Missing type pool does not create false candidates
notes: An empty TV exact pool remains an explicit scarcity instead of a fabricated result.

title: Cross-media supplement preserves seed relationship
notes: Cross-media candidates carry both the seed identity and transferable genre evidence.

title: Adaptive recall list budget remains eight
notes: Recall breadth comes from scheduling rather than a larger list budget.

title: Detail allocation remains sixteen
notes: Semantic detail fairness stays within the existing detail ceiling.

title: High-score duplicate wins over lower-score duplicate
notes: Scoring precedes display-title deduplication.

title: Recommendation reason matches actual candidate source and genre
notes: Direct and cross-media reasons retain their real seed and genre provenance.

title: Multi-seed common candidates cannot starve a selected type
notes: Common movie candidates cannot consume all primary slots before global drama coverage is reserved.

title: Three-seed and three-type global coverage
notes: Seed representation and three-type reservation are solved by one global allocator.

title: Seed and type reservation shares one slot
notes: A candidate satisfying both reservations is selected once and consumes one primary slot.

title: Selected genre preserves transferable seed evidence
notes: Selected SF and transferable Seed SF are independently preserved and validated.

title: Conflicting selected genre requires both contracts
notes: A Romance TV candidate is rejected when it lacks transferable SF relationship evidence.

title: Skipped cross-media request does not increment request count
notes: No transferable Seed genre means no tmdbGet and no false request-issued diagnostic.

title: Provider and display media diagnostics remain separate
notes: Provider media counts use movie/tv while display content counts use movie/drama/animation.
